title: Styling Patterns and Theming
description: Organise styles, create themes, and use styled components.

slides.title: Organising Styles 🗂️
slides.content:
# Keep Styles Manageable

As apps grow, styles can become messy. Common patterns:

- **Separate StyleSheets** – keep styles in the same file but grouped.
- **Dedicated style files** – e.g., `HomeScreen.styles.js`.
- **Global constants** – define colours, spacing, typography in one place.
- **Theming** – support light/dark modes.

slides.title: Creating a Theme
Using the Theme

quizData.question: What is a benefit of using a theme system?

options.text: Consistent styling and easy dark mode

quizData.explanation: Theming centralises style constants, making it easy to maintain consistency and support multiple themes.

slides.title: Styled Components (Optional)
Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: For large apps, consider using `StyleSheet.flatten` or `Array` of styles for conditional styling. E.g., `style={[styles.base, isActive && styles.active]}`.
